mechanism: BigBang provisions the platform from a defined initial state (Entra ID app registrations, GitHub App, initial tenant config) to fully functioning by sequencing all capability deployments. A destroy workflow resets to that initial state. Both run weekly against the test hierarchy.
why: A platform without a verified destroy/recreate cycle accumulates untracked state — bootstrap failures go undetected in test and surface in production.

related.note: Independent capabilities can be composed into a sequence without coupling.
deleteAll on each stack makes destroy a complete reset.

violations: Resource provisioned by BigBang but not removed by destroy — pollutes initial state on next run.
New capability added without a BigBang job — excluded from bootstrap.
Resource belonging to manual initial state provisioned by BigBang — blurs the boundary.
